# Concepts — ENG domain

<!-- Auto-generated by JOS charter sync. DO NOT EDIT. -->
<!-- Generated at: 2026-07-10T00:00:20.377534+00:00 -->

This index lists every JOS-canonical concept declared under the `eng` domain. Source of truth: jordan-operating-system repo.

**Count:** 9

| ID | Title | Stage | Flexibility | Summary |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [JOS-C13](JOS-C13-graceful-degradation.md) | Graceful Degradation & Antifragility | adopt |  | Explain why partial failure is preferable to total failure, and why systems can be designed to grow stronger from stress rather than merely surviving it. |
| [JOS-C20](JOS-C20-composable-architecture.md) | Composable Architecture | adopt |  | Explain why complex systems should be built from simple, interchangeable components. |
| [JOS-C21](JOS-C21-system-entropy.md) | System Entropy | adopt |  | Explain why engineered systems naturally drift toward disorder without active maintenance. |
| [JOS-C22](JOS-C22-feedback-loop-latency.md) | Feedback Loop Latency | adopt |  | Explain why the time between an action and its observable consequence determines system governability. |
| [JOS-C23](JOS-C23-failure-inevitability.md) | Failure Inevitability | adopt |  | Explain why failure is a statistical certainty in complex systems, not an avoidable exception. |
| [JOS-C24](JOS-C24-cognitive-load-budgeting.md) | Cognitive Load Budgeting | adopt |  | Explain why human attention and comprehension capacity are finite engineering constraints. |
| [JOS-C25](JOS-C25-abstraction-leakage.md) | Abstraction Leakage | adopt |  | Explain why no abstraction perfectly hides its implementation, and why this matters for system design. |
| [JOS-C26](JOS-C26-change-as-primary-risk.md) | Change as Primary Risk | adopt |  | Explain why the act of changing a system is the dominant source of production incidents. |
| [JOS-C27](JOS-C27-interfaces-outlive-implementations.md) | Interfaces Outlive Implementations | adopt |  | Explain why the contracts between components persist longer than the components themselves. |
